title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Oh, you're going to keep adding on, huh?
You're going to bring your shovel?
I'm going to be wide awake tonight.
Don't try to think that you're going to, if you work 24 hours a day, you're going to get ahead because you're not.
You're going to destroy your body, so just relax.

title: will / shall
examples: Well, maybe we'll have to charge him something for the tree.
I'll shovel us out.
Well, they'll wake you up when they run across your face.
Every once in a while, you'll see a bear.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: Then before you start building, you've got to have water.
Then when you get the water, then you can start thinking about what you want to build, how big a cabin do I want.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: Can you give some relationship advice?
I could run around on that roof, but I have a lot of help to put it on.
I could sell this for lots of money.
Because I can imagine back in the 90s.
Level everything off so that you can build there.

title: have to / must
examples: Well, maybe we'll have to charge him something for the tree.
If you need help, all you have to do is mention it to somebody and they come.
First, you have to buy the property.
Then you have to have somebody come with a bulldozer.
So now that you can have to cement people come and put a foundation in to bring it up where you want it.

title: mustn't / needn't
examples: I don't need to know this grandpa.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: And in the winter we would come up with snow machines with a family and with the little kids and the grandkids like you.
Up in the woods, and then on the weekends, my wife, Shirley, she would come.
And she would help me for two days.
Then she'd kiss me on the cheek and wave and go, bye-bye.
She'd go to say, agonaut.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: One day, Grandma and I was out on a trail, and we hear the dogs barking in all of a sudden.

title: Passive (special: get + p.p.)
examples: Hope we don't get stuck, right?
Because you get tired of going to the outhouse.
